draft.title: a CRM for freelancers
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a CRM for freelancers

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer scannable contact cards over dense data tables for quick client overview
facade.axisTargeted: dense data vs key metrics — table layout vs card layout for client roster
facade.label: Client list as cards
facade.acceptImplies: explore card-based UI for all client views; prioritize visual hierarchy and white space over information density
facade.rejectImplies: explore tabular/spreadsheet-style layouts; users want to see many data points at once; prioritize compact grid views

evidence.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer scannable contact cards over dense data tables for quick client overview
evidence.implication: explore card-based UI for all client views; prioritize visual hierarchy and white space over information density

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer high contrast layouts that separate navigation from work areas
facade.label: Dark sidebar, light content
facade.acceptImplies: Explore dark UI patterns, strong contrast hierarchy, dashboard-style layouts
facade.rejectImplies: Move toward softer backgrounds, monochromatic palettes, less visual separation

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer a persistent navigation sidebar (clients, projects, invoices) paired with a card-based list view for quick scanning of active work.
facade.label: Sidebar with card list
facade.acceptImplies: Explore fixed sidebars with medium-density card layouts for core workflows
facade.rejectImplies: Test full-width layouts, tab-based navigation, or denser list-style alternatives

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer animated panel transitions over instant visibility changes because it creates continuity and reduces cognitive load when switching between clients or projects
facade.axisTargeted: Animated transitions vs instant state changes
facade.label: Smooth slide-in
facade.acceptImplies: Build motion-heavy interactions throughout the CRM: sliding panels, morphing buttons, fade-in lists, eased state changes
facade.rejectImplies: Prioritize instant feedback and snappy interactions: no-animation UI, immediate results on click, direct jumps between views

facade.hypothesis: CRM for freelancers benefits from approachable, human-centered warmth rather than corporate coldness — soft colors, friendly microcopy, and inviting interface that feels like a trusted assistant rather than a sterile tool
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward intimate, human-first design; probably wants the CRM to feel like a collaborator, not a database. Suggests fondness for cream/amber/rust tones, rounded edges, conversational language.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clarity and efficiency over comfort; may want crisp, minimal, or professional-cool aesthetic. Doesn't need the CRM to feel 'friendly' — wants it sharp and no-nonsense.

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers prefer friendly, informal language in their CRM tools because it feels less corporate and more like talking to a peer or assistant
facade.label: Casual, conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: The voice should be warm, use contractions, ask questions, celebrate wins — feels like a supportive colleague
facade.rejectImplies: The voice should be formal, corporate, procedural — feels like official business software

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/dashboard vs. spread across tabs and modals—reveals navigation preference for a freelancer juggling multiple clients
Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive peer
Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals stress tolerance and work style for solo freelancers
Whether user prefers data shown as numbers/tables vs. visual summaries and charts—reveals how they want to understand client relationships and project status at a glance
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. smooth, animated transitions—reveals if they value speed/snappiness or prefer thoughtful, guided interactions when managing clients

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean aesthetics vs. rich visual hierarchy with color/imagery—reveals if they want a 'blank canvas' CRM or one with visual warmth and personality
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools clustered in one sidebar/dashboard vs. spread across tabs and modals—reveals navigation preference for a freelancer juggling multiple clients
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to professional/formal tone vs. casual/encouraging tone—reveals if they want a business-like assistant or a supportive peer
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm, distraction-free workspace vs. energetic, motivational environment—reveals stress tolerance and work style for solo freelancers
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data shown as numbers/tables vs. visual summaries and charts—reveals how they want to understand client relationships and project status at a glance
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. smooth, animated transitions—reveals if they value speed/snappiness or prefer thoughtful, guided interactions when managing clients
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Freelancers track project phases and deadlines better with chronological views than spreadsheet-style data tables
facade.axisTargeted: INFORMATION DESIGN — temporal narrative vs static grid
facade.label: Timeline over table
facade.acceptImplies: Explore timeline/pipeline views for projects; prioritize chronological progression and milestones over columnar data; add Gantt-style or phase-based layouts
facade.rejectImplies: Stick with table-based project views; users need to scan all columns at once; chronological story is secondary to quick fact lookup
